phases.name: backup
phases.prompt: Write 'backup.sh', a bash script that takes a source directory as its first argument and creates a gzipped tarball named 'backup-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.tar.gz' in the current directory. It must start with '#!/usr/bin/env bash', use 'set -euo pipefail', print usage and exit non-zero if no argument is given, and echo the name of the archive it created. Output ONLY the complete contents of backup.sh inside a single ```bash code block, nothing else.

phases.name: restore

scorers.rubric: restore.sh is a valid bash script that extracts a backup-*.tar.gz archive (matching the format produced by the provided backup.sh) into an optional target directory and validates its arguments.
